Accountability  At a bare-knuckled press event in Times Square in early January, former New York City Mayor Eric Adams stepped onto a stage not to champion traffic reforms or housing plans but to sell a cryptocurrency.  Branded simply as NYC Token, the digital coin was pitched as a novel vehicle to “fight antisemitism and anti-Americanism,” fund scholarships, and usher everyday New Yorkers into the age of blockchain. Adams, a longtime crypto booster who once drew pay in Bitcoin as mayor, presented the project with the flair of a visionary pivoting from public office to private innovation.  But the euphoria was short-lived.  Within minutes of debuting on public markets, NYC Token’s valuation soared into the hundreds of millions, only to crater by more than 80 percent...</div></div></div></div><div class="td-block-span12"><div class="td_module_10 td_module_wrap td-animation-stack"><div class="td-module-thumb"><a href="https://techgeeked.com/sebastian-stan-two-face-and-the-high-stakes-future-of-the-batman-part-ii/" rel="bookmark" class="td-image-wrap" title="Sebastian Stan, Two-Face, and the High-Stakes Future of The Batman: Part II"><img data-recalc-dims="1" loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="180" height="135" class="entry-thumb" src="https://i0.wp.com/techgeeked.com/wp-content/themes/Newsmag/images/no-thumb/td_180x135.png?resize=180%2C135&#038;ssl=1"  alt="" /></a></div><div class="item-details"><h3 class="entry-title td-module-title"><a href="https://techgeeked.com/sebastian-stan-two-face-and-the-high-stakes-future-of-the-batman-part-ii/" rel="bookmark" title="Sebastian Stan, Two-Face, and the High-Stakes Future of The Batman: Part II">Sebastian Stan, Two-Face, and the High-Stakes Future of The Batman: Part II</a></h3><div class="meta-info"> <span class="td-post-author-name"><a href="https://techgeeked.com/author/j-ableman/">James Ableman</a> <span>-</span> </span> <span class="td-post-date"><time class="entry-date updated td-module-date" datetime="2026-01-09T10:56:19+00:00" >January 9, 2026</time></span><div class="td-module-comments"><a href="https://techgeeked.com/sebastian-stan-two-face-and-the-high-stakes-future-of-the-batman-part-ii/#respond">0</a></div></div><div class="td-excerpt"> In a genre built on secrets, rumors are currency, and right now, few are circulating louder than the idea of Sebastian Stan joining The Batman: Part II. The speculation, sparked by industry chatter and amplified by carefully scrubbed social media posts, has ignited a familiar but telling debate: not just who Stan might play, but what his casting would signal for Batman, DC Studios, and a superhero genre desperate to evolve without alienating its base.  The leading theory that Stan could portray Harvey Dent, eventually becoming Two-Face, isn’t surprising. Two-Face is one of Batman’s most tragic villains, a symbol of moral collapse rather than cosmic chaos. And in Matt Reeves’ Gotham, a city soaked in corruption, rot, and institutional failure. Harvey Dent feels less like a rehash and more like an inevitability.  But inevitability cuts both ways.</div></div></div></div><div class="td-block-span12"><div class="td_module_10 td_module_wrap td-animation-stack"><div class="td-module-thumb"><a Instead, it broadens the world, deepens its characters, and leans into the game lore in ways that feel both bold and surprisingly meaningful. Suddenly, after years of jockeying for position, it looks like Google is catching up and maybe even overtaking.  🔄 From Follower to Formidable Rival  In 2022, when ChatGPT exploded onto the scene, OpenAI caught competitors off guard. The launch of a powerful, widely accessible generative‑AI tool jolted the industry. Google responded with alarm: previously, its own “code red” had reorganized teams and accelerated AI development in response to ChatGPT’s arrival.  Fast‑forward three years, and the roles are reversed. there’s a consistent theme: if something can be turned into a market, it will be. His vision, he said, is to “ any difference in opinion” by turning every disagreement, prediction, or point of uncertainty into a tradable contract. The short-form video giant has announced a partnership with iHeartMedia to launch the TikTok Podcast Network, a new audio initiative designed to bring popular creators into podcasting. The collaboration will feature up to 25 creator-driven shows, distributed across iHeartRadio’s digital platforms and broadcast channels.  It’s a bold move that signals TikTok’s ambitions to expand beyond the feed and establish itself as a true multi-platform entertainment brand.  From Scroll to Streams  The partnership goes beyond podcasting. TikTok and iHeartMedia plan to open co-branded podcast studios in Los Angeles, New York, and Atlanta, giving creators access to professional audio resources and production support. Pluribus, his highly anticipated return to television, takes viewers to a place both familiar and deeply strange: a world where nearly all of humanity has merged into a single consciousness, leaving only a handful of “unassimilated” individuals to navigate what’s left of civilization.  The two-episode premiere, which dropped November 7, 2025, isn’t a bombastic sci-fi spectacle. Instead, it’s a quiet storm, a slow, unnerving meditation on power, individuality, and what it means to stay human when humanity itself has fused into one.  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a6lzvWby9UE  A High-Concept Premise That Actually Works  Gilligan, best known for Breaking Bad and Better Call Saul, approaches science fiction with the same meticulous eye for human frailty that defined his earlier work.
